meta.title: Universal Math Curriculum Skill Tree
meta.description: A detailed, curriculum-agnostic taxonomy of K-12 math skills organized by domain, unit, and sub-skill. Each skill includes a typical grade range, prerequisites, and difficulty scaling notes. Designed to power a universal math worksheet/question generator.

schema.domain: Top-level math strand (e.g., Number & Operations, Algebra)
schema.unit: A coherent topic within a domain (e.g., Fraction Operations)
schema.skill: A specific, assessable ability (e.g., Add fractions with unlike denominators)
schema.grade_range: [min, max] — typical grades where this skill is taught
schema.prerequisites: List of skill IDs that should be mastered first

difficulty_levels.2: Developing — small numbers, may require 2 steps
difficulty_levels.3: Proficient — moderate numbers, multi-step, some reasoning
difficulty_levels.4: Advanced — larger numbers, multi-step, applied/word problems
difficulty_levels.5: Challenge — complex scenarios, non-routine, proof-like or competition-style

schema.question_types: Suggested formats: computation, word_problem, visual, multiple_choice, true_false, fill_in_blank, matching, error_analysis, open_response

domains.description: Understanding of number systems, place value, and arithmetic operations across all number types.

skills.difficulty_notes: L1: count to 5, L2: count to 10, L3: count to 20, L4: count to 50, L5: count to 100
L1: count by 1s to 30, L2: to 50, L3: to 100, L4: count by 1s from any starting number, L5: count by 1s across century boundaries (98, 99, 100, 101)
L1: count up to 3 objects, L2: up to 5, L3: up to 10, L4: scattered arrangement, L5: objects in different sizes/overlapping
L1: organized rows up to 12, L2: up to 15, L3: up to 20, L4: scattered arrangement, L5: grouped and scattered mixed
L1: match 1-3 objects to numerals, L2: 4-5 objects, L3: 6-10, L4: identify errors in counting, L5: abstract representations

skills.skill_name: Count forward from a given number
skills.difficulty_notes: L1: start from single digit, L2: start from teens, L3: start from any two-digit, L4: count across decade boundary, L5: count across century boundary

skills.skill_name: Count backward from a given number
skills.difficulty_notes: L1: from 5, L2: from 10, L3: from 20, L4: across decade boundary, L5: from any two-digit number
L1: from 0 to 20, L2: from 0 to 50, L3: from any even number, L4: from any odd number, L5: backward skip counting by 2s
L1: from 0 to 50, L2: from 0 to 100, L3: from any multiple of 5, L4: from any number, L5: backward skip counting by 5s
L1: from 0 to 100, L2: from any multiple of 10, L3: from any number, L4: past 100, L5: backward skip counting by 10s
L1: by 3s from 0, L2: by 4s from 0, L3: mixed skip counts, L4: from non-zero starting points, L5: backward and identifying patterns
L1: with visual supports, L2: numerals only adjacent numbers, L3: any two numbers 0-10, L4: three-way comparison, L5: comparing quantities in word problems
L1: single-digit numbers, L2: two-digit numbers, L3: three-digit numbers, L4: numbers with different digit counts, L5: word problems requiring comparison

skills.skill_name: Order numbers from least to greatest / greatest to least
skills.difficulty_notes: L1: 3 single-digit numbers, L2: 4 two-digit numbers, L3: 5 three-digit numbers, L4: mixed digit counts, L5: ordering with negative numbers or decimals
L1: same denominator, L2: same numerator, L3: benchmark fractions (1/2), L4: unlike denominators requiring common denominator, L5: mixed numbers and improper fractions
L1: tenths only, L2: tenths and hundredths, L3: mixed decimal places, L4: decimals with trailing zeros, L5: ordering fractions and decimals together

skills.skill_name: Compare and order integers (positive and negative)

L1: represent with base-10 blocks, L2: identify tens/ones digit, L3: write in expanded form, L4: compose/decompose two-digit numbers, L5: explain why 32 ≠ 23
L1: identify digit values, L2: expanded form, L3: compose/decompose, L4: compare using place value, L5: word problems involving place value reasoning
L1: identify digit values up to thousands, L2: expanded form, L3: up to hundred-thousands, L4: compare multi-digit numbers using place value, L5: rounding and estimating
L1: read/write to millions, L2: expanded form, L3: to billions, L4: compare and order large numbers, L5: real-world context (population, distance)
L1: identify tenths, L2: identify hundredths, L3: thousandths, L4: expanded form with decimals, L5: place value relationships (0.1 = 10 × 0.01)
L1: round to nearest 10, L2: round to nearest 100, L3: round to nearest 1000, L4: round to any place, L5: use rounding for estimation in multi-step problems
L1: round to nearest whole, L2: round to nearest tenth, L3: round to nearest hundredth, L4: round to any decimal place, L5: estimation with decimal operations

skills.skill_name: Powers of 10 and place value patterns
skills.difficulty_notes: L1: multiply by 10, 100, 1000, L2: divide by 10, 100, 1000, L3: explain digit shifts, L4: multiply/divide decimals by powers of 10, L5: scientific notation intro
L1: with objects/pictures, L2: with number line, L3: symbolic only, L4: find missing addend, L5: word problems
L1: with visual supports, L2: symbolic, L3: fluency (timed), L4: missing addend, L5: word problems with distractors

L1: multiples of 10, L2: two-digit + one-digit, L3: two-digit + two-digit, L4: three addends, L5: word problems
L1: regroup ones only, L2: any two-digit + two-digit, L3: three addends, L4: estimate then calculate, L5: multi-step word problems
L1: no regrouping, L2: regroup ones, L3: regroup ones and tens, L4: multiple addends, L5: estimate and verify

skills.skill_name: Add multi-digit whole numbers (up to millions)
skills.difficulty_notes: L1: four-digit no regrouping, L2: four-digit with regrouping, L3: five+ digits, L4: multiple addends, L5: real-world multi-step
L1: demonstrate commutative with small numbers, L2: use associative to make 10, L3: identify properties by name, L4: apply properties to simplify, L5: justify using properties
L1: doubles, L2: near doubles, L3: make a 10, L4: compensation (add 99 = add 100 - 1), L5: break apart and combine multiple strategies

L1: with visual, L2: symbolic, L3: fluency, L4: missing numbers, L5: comparison word problems
L1: no regrouping, L2: with regrouping, L3: fluency, L4: fact families, L5: multi-step comparison word problems
L1: subtract multiples of 10, L2: two-digit minus one-digit, L3: two-digit minus two-digit, L4: word problems, L5: estimate then subtract

L1: no regrouping, L2: regroup ones, L3: regroup tens, L4: regroup across zeros (e.g., 400-167), L5: multi-step word problems
L1: four-digit simple, L2: with multiple regroupings, L3: across zeros, L4: five+ digits, L5: real-world multi-step
L1: complete fact families within 10, L2: within 20, L3: use addition to check subtraction, L4: missing number equations, L5: write own fact families and explain

skills.difficulty_notes: L1: model with pictures (2 groups of 3), L2: write as repeated addition, L3: write as multiplication, L4: word problems, L5: arrays and area models
L1: identity property, L2: zero property, L3: mixed, L4: explain why, L5: apply in larger expressions

L1: ×3 facts, L2: ×4 facts, L3: ×6 facts, L4: mixed fluency, L5: word problems and patterns

skills.difficulty_notes: L1: share with pictures, L2: write as division, L3: relate to multiplication, L4: word problems (partitive), L5: word problems (quotitive/measurement)

skills.difficulty_notes: L1: ÷1, ÷2, ÷5, ÷10, L2: ÷3, ÷4, ÷6, L3: ÷7, ÷8, ÷9, L4: fluency all facts, L5: missing dividend/divisor

L1: no remainder (84÷4), L2: with remainder, L3: zero in quotient, L4: three-digit dividends, L5: word problems
L1: two-digit ÷ two-digit, L2: three-digit ÷ two-digit, L3: four-digit ÷ two-digit, L4: with decimal quotients, L5: multi-step word problems

L1: divisible by 2, 5, 10, L2: divisible by 3, 9, L3: divisible by 4, 6, L4: apply multiple rules, L5: explain why rules work

skills.skill_name: Convert between fractions, decimals, and percents
skills.difficulty_notes: L1: common equivalents (1/2 = 0.5 = 50%), L2: any fraction to percent, L3: percent to fraction (simplify), L4: repeating decimals as percents, L5: order mixed representations

skills.skill_name: Find a percent of a number
skills.difficulty_notes: L1: 10%, 25%, 50% of whole numbers, L2: any percent of whole number, L3: percent of a decimal, L4: multi-step (find 15% tip), L5: percent of a percent

skills.skill_name: Find what percent one number is of another
skills.difficulty_notes: L1: simple (what % is 3 of 12), L2: non-whole result, L3: word problems, L4: comparing percentages, L5: multi-step analysis

skills.skill_name: Find the whole given a part and percent
skills.difficulty_notes: L1: simple (30 is 50% of what?), L2: non-benchmark percents, L3: word problems, L4: multi-step, L5: reverse percent problems
L1: calculate increase amount, L2: calculate new amount after increase, L3: decrease, L4: find the percent change, L5: successive percent changes
L1: simple discount, L2: sales tax, L3: tip calculation, L4: markup/markdown, L5: combined (discount + tax)
L1: describe ratio with visuals, L2: write ratios (a:b, a/b, a to b), L3: part-to-part vs part-to-whole, L4: equivalent ratios, L5: ratio word problems
L1: complete ratio table, L2: find missing values, L3: generate equivalent ratios, L4: word problems, L5: double number lines
L1: find unit rate from table, L2: calculate unit rate, L3: compare using unit rates, L4: unit pricing, L5: complex unit rates (miles per gallon, etc.)
L1: find missing value in proportion, L2: set up proportion from word problem, L3: multi-step, L4: with fractions/decimals, L5: indirect measurement and scale
L1: use given scale to find actual, L2: find scale factor, L3: create scale drawing, L4: area and scale, L5: multi-step map problems
L1: identify direct proportion, L2: write equation y = kx, L3: identify inverse proportion, L4: write equation y = k/x, L5: distinguish and solve mixed problems

skills.skill_name: Order of operations without exponents (BEDMAS/PEMDAS basics)
skills.difficulty_notes: L1: two operations no parentheses, L2: with parentheses, L3: three operations, L4: nested parentheses, L5: error analysis (find the mistake)
L1: simple with one exponent, L2: exponents and multiplication, L3: full BEDMAS, L4: with fractions and decimals, L5: complex nested expressions

skills.skill_name: Order of operations with integers and rational numbers
skills.difficulty_notes: L1: integers with two operations, L2: integers with parentheses, L3: rationals mixed, L4: absolute value in expressions, L5: multi-step complex expressions

skills.skill_name: Classify numbers as natural, whole, integer, rational, or irrational
skills.difficulty_notes: L1: identify natural and whole numbers, L2: classify rational numbers (fractions, terminating/repeating decimals), L3: identify irrational numbers (√2, π), L4: Venn diagram of number sets and subsets, L5: prove a number is irrational (e.g., √2) or explain why repeating decimals are rational

skills.skill_name: Understand the real number system and its properties
skills.difficulty_notes: L1: place numbers on a number line (including irrationals), L2: density property (between any two reals is another), L3: closure under operations (rationals closed under +, ×; irrationals not), L4: identify what set a result belongs to (rational + irrational = irrational), L5: introduction to complex/imaginary numbers as extending beyond reals

skills.skill_name: Earning and payment (coins, bills, making change)
skills.difficulty_notes: L1: identify coins and bills, L2: count mixed coins to make an amount, L3: make change from a purchase, L4: word problems with multiple items, L5: compare costs and budgeting basics
L1: identify income and expenses, L2: create a simple budget, L3: track spending and compare to budget, L4: adjust budget when income/expenses change, L5: savings goals and priorities

skills.skill_name: Gross and net pay (income, deductions, taxes)
skills.difficulty_notes: L1: calculate gross pay (hourly rate × hours), L2: overtime pay calculations, L3: identify common deductions (CPP, EI, income tax), L4: calculate net pay from gross pay and deductions, L5: compare pay structures (hourly, salary, commission, piecework) and calculate annual income
L1: calculate interest I = Prt, L2: find total amount A = P + I, L3: find rate or time, L4: compare two simple interest scenarios, L5: word problems with mixed unknowns

L1: annual compounding A = P(1+r)^t, L2: semi-annual and quarterly (A = P(1+r/n)^(nt)), L3: monthly compounding, L4: find rate or time (using logarithms), L5: compare simple vs compound interest, continuous compounding A = Pe^(rt)

L1: future value of annuity (regular deposits, using technology/formula), L2: present value of annuity, L3: determine regular payment amount, L4: RRSP/savings plan scenarios, L5: compare investment options with different compounding and payment structures

L1: calculate total cost of a loan (principal + interest), L2: monthly payment calculation (using technology), L3: amortization concepts (how much goes to interest vs principal), L4: mortgage scenarios (down payment, amortization period, payment frequency), L5: compare loan options and total interest paid over different terms

L1: compare total cost of buying vs leasing (simple), L2: factor in depreciation, L3: renting vs buying a home (opportunity cost), L4: car lease terms (residual value, mileage), L5: comprehensive scenario — make and justify a financial decision using multiple factors

domains.description: Patterns, variables, expressions, equations, inequalities, and functions from early algebraic thinking through advanced algebra.

skills.skill_name: Identify and extend repeating patterns (AB, ABC, ABB, etc.)
skills.difficulty_notes: L1: two-element patterns, L2: three-element, L3: growing patterns, L4: find the rule, L5: create own patterns
L1: find next in +1, +2, +5, +10, L2: find rule, L3: decreasing patterns, L4: two-step rules (×2+1), L5: extend and explain pattern
L1: one operation rule, L2: two operation rule, L3: find the rule from table, L4: complete table both directions, L5: write equation from table
L1: find common difference, L2: find nth term, L3: find n given a term, L4: sum of arithmetic series, L5: word problems and proofs
L1: find common ratio, L2: find nth term, L3: find n given a term, L4: sum of geometric series (finite), L5: infinite geometric series and convergence
L1: _ + 3 = 7, L2: use letter variables, L3: variable in different positions, L4: two unknowns, L5: explain what a variable represents
L1: one-operation expressions, L2: two-operation, L3: evaluate for given value, L4: translate words to expressions, L5: evaluate with multiple variables
L1: combine single variable terms, L2: with constants, L3: multiple variables, L4: with parentheses, L5: with fractions and decimals as coefficients
L1: a(b+c) with numbers, L2: with variables, L3: negative outside, L4: multiple distributions, L5: factor out common factors (reverse)
L1: factor out a number, L2: factor out a variable, L3: factor out monomial, L4: factor by grouping, L5: multi-step factoring
L1: x + a = b (positive), L2: x - a = b, L3: with negative results, L4: with fractions/decimals, L5: word problems → equation → solve
L1: ax = b (whole numbers), L2: x/a = b, L3: with negatives, L4: with fractions, L5: word problems
L1: ax + b = c, L2: x/a + b = c, L3: with negatives, L4: with fractions, L5: word problems

skills.skill_name: Solve multi-step equations (variables on both sides)
skills.difficulty_notes: L1: ax + b = cx + d, L2: with distribution, L3: no solution / infinite solutions, L4: with fractions, L5: complex multi-step with word problems
L1: solve for one variable (d=rt for t), L2: formulas with fractions, L3: quadratic formulas, L4: multiple occurrences of target variable, L5: complex scientific formulas

skills.skill_name: Understand inequality symbols and graph on number line
skills.difficulty_notes: L1: read inequality statements, L2: graph simple inequalities, L3: write inequality from number line, L4: compound inequalities, L5: real-world interpretation
L1: add/subtract inequalities, L2: multiply/divide (positive), L3: multiply/divide (negative — flip!), L4: graph solution set, L5: word problems
L1: multi-step single inequality, L2: AND compound, L3: OR compound, L4: absolute value inequalities, L5: systems of inequalities intro

L1: x² - a² > 0 (factorable, positive leading coeff), L2: x² + bx + c < 0 (two real roots), L3: ax² + bx + c ≥ 0 (a≠1), L4: sign analysis with number line / test intervals, L5: no real roots or always positive/negative, quadratic inequality word problems

L1: (x-a)/(x-b) > 0 (simple), L2: identify critical values (zeros and non-permissible), L3: sign chart with rational expression, L4: combined polynomial and rational factors, L5: multi-step with domain restrictions and word problems

L1: write solution in interval notation (single interval), L2: union of intervals, L3: sign chart for polynomial, L4: sign chart for rational, L5: identify where f(x) > 0, f(x) < 0, f(x) = 0 from graph or equation
L1: |x| = a, L2: |ax + b| = c, L3: |ax + b| = |cx + d|, L4: extraneous solutions check, L5: absolute value equations with quadratics inside
L1: |x| < a (conjunction), L2: |x| > a (disjunction), L3: |ax + b| ≤ c, L4: |ax + b| ≥ c, L5: compound absolute value with interval notation

L1: graph y = |x| and identify shape, L2: graph y = |ax + b|, L3: transformations of absolute value functions, L4: piecewise definition of absolute value, L5: analyze y = |f(x)| for quadratic and linear f(x), identify domain/range/intercepts
L1: identify degree, L2: classify by terms (monomial/binomial/trinomial), L3: standard form, L4: leading coefficient, L5: identify from context
L1: add monomials, L2: add binomials, L3: add trinomials, L4: subtract polynomials, L5: multi-step with distribution
L1: monomial × polynomial, L2: binomial × binomial (FOIL), L3: binomial × trinomial, L4: polynomial × polynomial, L5: special products (difference of squares, perfect squares)
L1: both factors positive, L2: one negative, L3: both negative, L4: with common factor first, L5: word problems and geometric applications
L1: decomposition method, L2: AC method, L3: with GCF factored first, L4: mixed practice, L5: complex coefficients

skills.skill_name: Factor special products (difference of squares, perfect square trinomials, sum/difference of cubes)
skills.difficulty_notes: L1: basic difference of squares (x²-9), L2: generalized difference of squares (a²x²-b²y², e.g., 25x²-16y²), L3: perfect square trinomials (x²+6x+9), L4: sum/difference of cubes (x³±8), L5: recognize in non-obvious forms (x⁴-1 as nested, or 9(x+1)²-4)

skills.skill_name: Factoring by substitution / pattern recognition: a(f(x))² + b(f(x)) + c
skills.difficulty_notes: L1: x⁴ + 5x² + 6 (let u = x²), L2: (x+1)² + 3(x+1) + 2, L3: with radicals or fractional exponents, L4: mixed — identify the substitution, L5: higher degree or nested patterns requiring multiple factoring steps

skills.skill_name: Polynomial long division and synthetic division
skills.difficulty_notes: L1: monomial divisor, L2: binomial divisor (no remainder), L3: with remainder, L4: synthetic division, L5: factor theorem and remainder theorem
L1: x² + bx + c = 0 (simple), L2: ax² + bx + c = 0, L3: requiring rearrangement, L4: word problems, L5: higher-degree equations reducible to quadratic

skills.skill_name: Solve quadratics using the quadratic formula
skills.difficulty_notes: L1: positive rational roots, L2: irrational roots, L3: complex roots, L4: word problems, L5: derive the formula by completing the square

skills.skill_name: Solve quadratics by completing the square
skills.difficulty_notes: L1: a=1, even b, L2: a=1, odd b, L3: a≠1, L4: convert to vertex form, L5: derive quadratic formula
L1: calculate discriminant, L2: determine number of real roots, L3: complex roots, L4: find k for specific root conditions, L5: graphical interpretation
L1: y = x² transformations, L2: vertex form y = a(x-h)²+k, L3: standard form → vertex, L4: identify axis of symmetry, vertex, intercepts, L5: applications (projectile motion)
L1: max/min from vertex, L2: projectile motion, L3: area optimization, L4: revenue/profit, L5: multi-constraint problems
L1: both in slope-intercept form, L2: mixed forms, L3: identify no solution (parallel), L4: infinite solutions, L5: estimate from graph and verify
L1: one variable isolated, L2: isolate then substitute, L3: with fractions, L4: word problems, L5: no solution / infinite solutions
L1: opposite coefficients, L2: multiply one equation, L3: multiply both, L4: with fractions/decimals, L5: word problems and special cases
L1: define variables and write system, L2: mixture problems, L3: distance-rate-time, L4: break-even, L5: three-variable systems
L1: graph two linear inequalities, L2: identify feasible region, L3: three or more inequalities, L4: optimization (linear programming intro), L5: real-world constraints

skills.skill_name: Plot points on the coordinate plane
skills.difficulty_notes: L1: first quadrant only, L2: all four quadrants, L3: identify coordinates from graph, L4: distance between points (horizontal/vertical), L5: reflections across axes
L1: find slope from graph (counting), L2: slope formula, L3: positive/negative/zero/undefined, L4: parallel and perpendicular slopes, L5: rate of change in context
L1: identify m and b, L2: graph from equation, L3: write equation from graph, L4: write equation from two points, L5: real-world modeling
L1: write in point-slope form, L2: convert to slope-intercept, L3: from two points, L4: parallel/perpendicular lines, L5: applications

skills.skill_name: Standard form of a linear equation (Ax + By = C)
skills.difficulty_notes: L1: identify standard form, L2: find intercepts, L3: convert between forms, L4: graph from standard form, L5: applications
L1: identify rate and starting value, L2: write equation, L3: make predictions, L4: compare two linear models, L5: interpret and critique models

L1: simplify perfect squares under radical, L2: simplify non-perfect (√50 = 5√2), L3: simplify with variables (√(50x³)), L4: rationalize denominator (monomial and binomial), L5: cube roots and higher index radicals with variables

skills.skill_name: Convert between radicals and rational exponents
skills.difficulty_notes: L1: √a = a^(1/2), L2: ⁿ√a = a^(1/n), L3: (√a)^m = a^(m/n), L4: simplify expressions using exponent laws with rational exponents, L5: evaluate numerical expressions both ways and determine which form is simpler

L1: compare √a and √b, L2: place radicals on number line (approximate), L3: order a mix of radicals, fractions, and decimals, L4: without calculator — use squaring to compare (e.g., 3√2 vs 2√5), L5: mixed forms including π, rational exponents

L1: add/subtract like radicals, L2: simplify then add/subtract, L3: multiply radicals (including distributive), L4: FOIL with radicals and conjugates, L5: divide and rationalize with binomial denominators

skills.skill_name: Solve radical equations (including domain restrictions and extraneous roots)
skills.difficulty_notes: L1: √x = a (check domain x≥0), L2: √(ax+b) = c (identify domain restriction), L3: isolate radical, square, check for extraneous solutions, L4: two radicals (isolate one, square, repeat), L5: equations mixing radical and linear terms, explain why extraneous roots arise

skills.skill_name: Graph radical functions and identify characteristics
skills.difficulty_notes: L1: graph y = √x and identify domain/range, L2: transformations y = a√(b(x-h))+k, L3: determine domain from expression under radical, L4: find intercepts algebraically, L5: solve graphically — intersection of radical and linear functions

L1: monomial denominator (x≠0), L2: binomial denominator (x≠a), L3: factored polynomial denominator, L4: state restrictions before simplifying, L5: restrictions from context/word problems (e.g., dimensions must be positive)

L1: monomial/monomial, L2: factor and cancel common binomial, L3: trinomial numerator/denominator, L4: factor both then cancel (state NPVs), L5: complex rational expressions (fractions within fractions)
L1: monomial, L2: factor then multiply, L3: divide (multiply by reciprocal), L4: multi-step with NPVs, L5: complex expressions
L1: same denominator, L2: find LCD (monomial), L3: LCD with polynomial denominators, L4: complex fractions, L5: word problems requiring addition/subtraction of rational expressions

skills.skill_name: Solve rational equations (including extraneous root identification)
skills.difficulty_notes: L1: variable in one denominator — multiply through, L2: variable in both denominators, L3: check for extraneous solutions (solution equals NPV), L4: work/rate problems, L5: mixture problems and multi-step applications

skills.skill_name: Understand function concept (definition, notation, vertical line test)
skills.difficulty_notes: L1: is it a function (sets of ordered pairs), L2: function notation f(x), L3: evaluate f(a), L4: domain and range from graph, L5: domain restrictions algebraically
L1: from a graph, L2: from equation (linear), L3: from quadratic/radical, L4: from rational, L5: piecewise and composite
L1: evaluate f(g(a)), L2: find f(g(x)), L3: find g(f(x)), L4: domain of composition, L5: decompose composite function
L1: find inverse of linear, L2: verify inverses, L3: inverse of quadratic (restricted domain), L4: graph inverse as reflection, L5: inverse of exponential/logarithmic
L1: vertical shifts, L2: horizontal shifts, L3: reflections, L4: stretches and compressions, L5: combined transformations and write equations
L1: evaluate piecewise, L2: graph piecewise, L3: write piecewise from graph, L4: domain and range, L5: absolute value as piecewise
L1: y = 2^x, L2: y = a·b^x, L3: identify growth vs decay, L4: transformations, L5: asymptotes and end behavior
L1: population growth, L2: radioactive decay, L3: compound interest, L4: find rate or time, L5: compare models
L1: convert exponential to log, L2: evaluate simple logs, L3: common and natural log, L4: log properties, L5: change of base formula
L1: expand using properties, L2: condense, L3: evaluate with properties, L4: prove identities, L5: apply to solve equations
L1: same base exponential, L2: using logs to solve, L3: log equations, L4: check extraneous, L5: application problems

domains.description: Shapes, spatial reasoning, measurement, coordinate geometry, transformations, and proofs.

skills.skill_name: Identify and describe 2D shapes (circle, triangle, square, rectangle)
skills.difficulty_notes: L1: name shapes, L2: describe attributes (sides, corners), L3: classify by attributes, L4: compare shapes, L5: composite shapes

skills.skill_name: Identify and describe 3D shapes (cube, sphere, cylinder, cone, prism)
skills.difficulty_notes: L1: name 3D shapes, L2: faces/edges/vertices, L3: relate to 2D shapes, L4: real-world examples, L5: nets of 3D shapes
L1: by sides (equilateral, isosceles, scalene), L2: by angles (acute, right, obtuse), L3: combine classifications, L4: from coordinates, L5: triangle inequality theorem
L1: identify (square, rectangle, parallelogram, rhombus, trapezoid), L2: properties of each, L3: hierarchy/relationships, L4: from coordinates, L5: prove classification

skills.skill_name: Polygons (regular and irregular, naming by sides)
skills.difficulty_notes: L1: name by number of sides (pentagon through decagon), L2: regular vs irregular, L3: interior angle sum, L4: exterior angles, L5: diagonals formula
L1: identify radius, diameter, center, L2: circumference formula, L3: chord, arc, sector, L4: central angles, L5: inscribed angles
L1: identify lines of symmetry, L2: draw lines of symmetry, L3: complete symmetric figure, L4: rotational symmetry and order, L5: symmetry in coordinate plane

skills.skill_name: Understand and classify angles (acute, right, obtuse, straight, reflex)
skills.difficulty_notes: L1: identify right angles, L2: classify acute/obtuse, L3: estimate angle measures, L4: straight and reflex, L5: angles in real-world contexts
L1: measure given angles, L2: draw angles of given measure, L3: reflex angles, L4: angles in shapes, L5: estimate then verify
L1: identify complementary pairs, L2: find complement, L3: identify supplementary pairs, L4: find supplement, L5: algebraic problems (find x)
L1: identify vertical angles, L2: find measures, L3: algebraic (find x), L4: multiple intersecting lines, L5: proofs

skills.skill_name: Angles formed by parallel lines and transversals
skills.difficulty_notes: L1: identify corresponding/alternate/co-interior, L2: find measures, L3: algebraic, L4: prove lines parallel, L5: complex multi-line configurations
L1: find missing angle (given two), L2: algebraic, L3: exterior angle theorem, L4: isosceles/equilateral angle problems, L5: proof of angle sum
L1: interior angle sum formula, L2: find missing angle, L3: regular polygon angles, L4: exterior angle sum (360°), L5: algebraic multi-step
L1: compare lengths, L2: measure with non-standard units, L3: rulers (inches/cm), L4: to nearest 1/2 inch or mm, L5: estimate and verify
L1: count grid units, L2: add given side lengths, L3: rectangles with formula, L4: irregular polygons, L5: find missing side given perimeter
L1: count unit squares, L2: formula l×w, L3: find missing dimension, L4: composite rectangular shapes, L5: word problems
L1: right triangles on grid, L2: formula 1/2 × b × h, L3: obtuse triangles, L4: find missing dimension, L5: composite shapes with triangles
L1: parallelogram (b×h), L2: trapezoid formula, L3: rhombus (diagonals), L4: composite shapes, L5: derive formulas from rectangles/triangles
L1: given radius, L2: given diameter, L3: find radius from circumference, L4: semicircles and arcs, L5: composite shapes
L1: given radius, L2: given diameter, L3: find radius from area, L4: sectors and semicircles, L5: composite shapes with circles

skills.skill_name: Surface area of prisms and cylinders
skills.difficulty_notes: L1: rectangular prism from net, L2: formula, L3: triangular prism, L4: cylinder, L5: composite solids

skills.skill_name: Surface area of pyramids, cones, spheres
skills.difficulty_notes: L1: square pyramid, L2: triangular pyramid, L3: cone, L4: sphere, L5: composite solids
L1: count unit cubes, L2: rectangular prism formula, L3: triangular prism, L4: cylinder, L5: word problems and composite solids
L1: pyramid, L2: cone, L3: sphere, L4: composite solids, L5: find dimensions given volume
L1: mm↔cm↔m, L2: m↔km, L3: mL↔L, g↔kg, L4: multi-step conversions, L5: area/volume unit conversions
L1: inches↔feet, L2: feet↔yards↔miles, L3: cups↔pints↔quarts↔gallons, L4: metric↔imperial, L5: multi-step with operations
L1: describe translation with visuals, L2: translate on coordinate plane, L3: write translation rule, L4: translate polygons, L5: composition of translations
L1: reflect across x-axis, L2: reflect across y-axis, L3: reflect across y=x, L4: reflect across any line, L5: identify line of reflection
L1: 90° rotations about origin, L2: 180° rotations, L3: 270° rotations, L4: any angle, L5: about points other than origin
L1: scale factor > 1 from origin, L2: scale factor < 1, L3: from other center points, L4: find scale factor, L5: similar figures and proportional reasoning
L1: identify congruent figures, L2: describe transformation sequence, L3: similar figures, L4: find missing measurements, L5: prove congruence/similarity
L1: Pythagorean triples (3-4-5), L2: non-integer results, L3: simplify radical, L4: word problems, L5: 3D applications
L1: whole number results, L2: irrational results, L3: word problems, L4: verify right triangles, L5: converse applications
L1: horizontal/vertical distances, L2: distance formula, L3: midpoint formula, L4: applications (perimeter on coordinate plane), L5: circle equations

skills.skill_name: Primary trigonometric ratios (SOH CAH TOA) in right triangles
skills.difficulty_notes: L1: identify opposite/adjacent/hypotenuse, L2: write ratios as fractions, L3: find missing side using trig ratio, L4: find missing angle using inverse trig, L5: word problems (angle of elevation/depression, indirect measurement)

L1: identify side ratios from diagram, L2: find missing sides using ratio relationships, L3: exact trig values for 30°, 45°, 60°, L4: applications using exact values, L5: derive the ratio relationships

skills.skill_name: Angles in standard position (terminal arm, initial arm, rotation)
skills.difficulty_notes: L1: draw angle in standard position given degree measure, L2: identify quadrant of terminal arm, L3: determine angle given a point on the terminal arm, L4: find exact trig ratios for any angle using coordinates of point on terminal arm (x, y, r), L5: angles greater than 360° and negative angles

L1: find reference angle for Q2, L2: find reference angle for Q3 and Q4, L3: use reference angle to determine exact trig ratios (with correct sign by CAST rule), L4: given a trig ratio value find all possible angles (0°-360°), L5: connect reference angles to special triangles for exact values in all four quadrants

L1: find positive coterminal angle (add 360°), L2: find negative coterminal angle (subtract 360°), L3: determine if two angles are coterminal, L4: find coterminal angle within a specific range, L5: general expression for all coterminal angles (θ + 360°n)

L1: key angles and coordinates on unit circle (0°, 90°, 180°, 270°, 360°), L2: coordinates for special angles (30°, 45°, 60° and equivalents in all quadrants), L3: sin/cos/tan from unit circle coordinates, L4: all six trig functions from unit circle, L5: evaluate expressions combining multiple trig values from unit circle
L1: convert common degree measures to radians (180° = π), L2: convert radians to degrees, L3: unit circle in radians, L4: arc length using radians (s = rθ), L5: sector area and angular velocity

L1: find a side using sine law (given AAS), L2: find an angle using sine law (given SSA — one solution), L3: ambiguous case — determine number of triangles (0, 1, or 2 solutions), L4: solve ambiguous case with two triangles, L5: contextual word problems requiring identification of ambiguous case

L1: find a side (given SAS), L2: find an angle (given SSS), L3: choose between sine law and cosine law, L4: multi-step problems (find one element, then use it to find another), L5: contextual word problems (navigation, surveying, indirect measurement)
L1: sin θ = a where a is a special value (0°-360°), L2: cos θ = a (0°-360°), L3: tan θ = a (0°-360°), L4: 2sin θ + 1 = 0 type (isolate first), L5: equations requiring factoring like sin²θ - sin θ = 0

skills.skill_name: Sine and cosine functions (graphing, amplitude, period, phase shift)
skills.difficulty_notes: L1: graph y = sin(x) and y = cos(x), L2: amplitude and vertical shift (y = a·sin(x) + d), L3: period (y = sin(bx)), L4: phase shift (y = sin(x - c)), L5: write equation from graph, model periodic phenomena
L1: graph y = tan(x) and identify asymptotes, L2: secant, cosecant, cotangent definitions, L3: graph reciprocal functions, L4: transformations, L5: domain restrictions and asymptotes

skills.skill_name: Trigonometric identities (Pythagorean, quotient, reciprocal, double angle)
skills.difficulty_notes: L1: Pythagorean identities (sin²θ + cos²θ = 1), L2: quotient and reciprocal identities, L3: verify/prove identities, L4: double angle formulas, L5: prove complex multi-step identities

L1: basic (sin x = 1/2) on [0, 2π), L2: general solution with nπ or 2nπ, L3: quadratic in trig function, L4: using identities to simplify then solve, L5: multi-step combining factoring, identities, and multiple angle formulas
L1: find midpoint, L2: find endpoint given midpoint, L3: verify midpoint, L4: midpoint applications, L5: median and centroid
L1: standard form given center and radius, L2: identify center and radius from equation, L3: general form to standard form, L4: intersections, L5: tangent lines

domains.description: Collecting, organizing, displaying, analyzing data, and understanding probability.

skills.skill_name: Collect and organize data (tally charts, frequency tables)
skills.difficulty_notes: L1: tally marks, L2: frequency tables, L3: design a survey, L4: two-way tables, L5: discuss bias in data collection
L1: read pictograph, L2: create pictograph, L3: read bar graph, L4: create bar graph, L5: interpret and compare
L1: read line plot, L2: create line plot, L3: with fractions, L4: describe distribution, L5: compare datasets
L1: read histogram, L2: create histogram, L3: choose intervals, L4: describe shape, L5: compare distributions
L1: read circle graph, L2: calculate percentages, L3: create circle graph, L4: central angles, L5: compare datasets
L1: plot points, L2: describe correlation (positive/negative/none), L3: draw line of best fit, L4: make predictions, L5: interpret slope and y-intercept in context
L1: read box plot, L2: create box plot, L3: compare two box plots, L4: identify outliers (IQR rule), L5: interpret in context
L1: read stem-and-leaf, L2: create, L3: back-to-back, L4: find statistics from plot, L5: compare datasets
L1: small dataset (3-5 values), L2: larger dataset, L3: find missing value given mean, L4: weighted mean, L5: effect of outliers
L1: median of odd dataset, L2: median of even dataset, L3: mode (one mode), L4: no mode / multiple modes, L5: compare mean vs median
L1: find range, L2: find range with negatives, L3: effect of outliers, L4: compare ranges, L5: use to assess spread
L1: find Q1, Q2, Q3, L2: calculate IQR, L3: identify outliers, L4: interpret in context, L5: compare datasets using IQR
L1: calculate MAD, L2: interpret MAD, L3: compare datasets, L4: word problems, L5: connect to standard deviation concept
L1: calculate variance (population), L2: calculate standard deviation, L3: sample vs population, L4: interpret in context, L5: normal distribution applications
L1: certain/impossible/likely, L2: express as fraction, L3: with visual models, L4: complement, L5: word problems
L1: equally likely outcomes (coins, dice), L2: express as fraction/decimal/percent, L3: not equally likely, L4: complement rule, L5: compare theoretical to experimental
L1: from given data, L2: conduct experiment, L3: compare to theoretical, L4: predict outcomes, L5: law of large numbers intro
L1: independent events (with replacement), L2: tree diagrams, L3: dependent events (without replacement), L4: multiplication rule, L5: three or more events
L1: fundamental counting principle, L2: factorial, L3: permutations (nPr), L4: combinations (nCr), L5: application in probability problems
L1: simple counting problems, L2: probability using combinations, L3: at least/at most problems, L4: conditional probability intro, L5: Bayes' theorem intro

domains.description: Foundational calculus concepts typically covered in Pre-Calculus 12 and introductory calculus courses.

skills.skill_name: Intuitive understanding of limits (tables and graphs)
skills.difficulty_notes: L1: estimate from graph, L2: estimate from table, L3: one-sided limits, L4: limits at infinity, L5: oscillating functions
L1: direct substitution, L2: factor and cancel, L3: rationalization, L4: special limits (sin x/x), L5: L'Hôpital's rule
L1: identify from graph, L2: three conditions for continuity, L3: removable vs jump vs infinite discontinuity, L4: piecewise continuity, L5: Intermediate Value Theorem

skills.skill_name: Average vs instantaneous rate of change
skills.difficulty_notes: L1: average rate from table, L2: average rate from function, L3: secant to tangent concept, L4: estimate instantaneous from graph, L5: difference quotient
L1: linear functions, L2: quadratic functions, L3: radical functions, L4: prove using definition, L5: piecewise differentiability
L1: power rule, L2: constant multiple, L3: sum/difference, L4: rewrite then differentiate, L5: higher-order derivatives
L1: product rule, L2: quotient rule, L3: combined, L4: with trig functions, L5: multi-step problems
L1: simple composite, L2: with trig, L3: with exponential, L4: multiple chain rule, L5: implicit differentiation

L1: reverse power rule, L2: with constant multiple, L3: sum/difference, L4: with initial conditions, L5: basic substitution

skills.skill_name: Definite integrals and area under a curve
skills.difficulty_notes: L1: Riemann sums (rectangles), L2: Fundamental Theorem of Calculus, L3: evaluate definite integrals, L4: area between curves, L5: applications (displacement, accumulation)
